name: Loop of the medieval villages of Sarrant and Hom
name_original: Village médiéval de Sarrant – boucle en terre gasconne
description: Cycle a difficult 51.8-mile loop through Gascony's rolling hills, vineyards, and medieval villages, centered on Sarrant.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Gimont offer a compelling experience through the Gers department's "slightly hilly" landscape. This region is characterized by rolling countryside, agricultural lands, and the renowned Armagnac vineyards, providing scenic vistas. The area features quiet roads with minimal traffic, making it suitable for exploring the Gascony countryside. The terrain includes manageable climbs, rewarding cyclists with expansive views. Best touring cycling routes around Gimont The most popular touring cycling route is Loop of the medieval villages of Sarrant and Hom, a 51.8 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic charm of medieval villages within the Gascony countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is L'Isle-Jourdain – A380 Oversized Route loop from Gimont-Cahuzac, a difficult 88.6 miles (142.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the region's rolling hills and open landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Aubiet, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the local agricultural lands, often completed in about 41 minutes. Touring cycling around Gimont is defined by rolling hills, Armagnac vineyards, and quiet country roads. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Gimont are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gimont's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling around Gimont?
A: The region around Gimont is characterized by a "slightly hilly" landscape, offering an engaging challenge for cyclists. You'll find rolling countryside, agricultural lands, and the renowned Armagnac vineyards, providing beautiful views. While there are climbs, they are generally manageable, especially with multi-geared bicycles or electric bikes.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Gimont area?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Gimont, catering to various skill levels. This includes a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult paths, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes near Gimont?
A: Yes, Gimont offers several easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35257463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Aubiet&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.8-mile (11.0 km) trail that takes you through local agricultural lands.

Q: What kind of natural features and landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Gimont?
A: Cyclists can enjoy picturesque scenery defined by rolling hills and agricultural lands, including the famous Armagnac vineyards. The area offers opportunities to breathe in fresh country air and discover landscapes of high amenity value. While not directly on every route, the wider Gers department boasts natural sites and local points of interest.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Several routes pass by interesting landmarks. For instance, you could cycle past &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6049788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caumont Castle&lt;/a&gt; or explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6049654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gimont Town Center&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thoux Saint-Cricq Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6381438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monferran-Savés&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any dedicated cycle paths or greenways in the Gimont region?
A: While many routes utilize quiet country roads, the broader Gers region, where Gimont is located, is known for its cycling infrastructure. Notable options include the Armagnac Greenway and cycle paths along the Banks of the Gers near Auch, providing dedicated routes for cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Gimont?
A: The touring cycling routes around Gimont are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, the beautiful views over the Armagnac vineyards, and the engaging, gently hilly terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Gimont?
A: The region offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for touring. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred during peak season.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39972379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain – A380 Oversized Route loop from Gimont-Cahuzac&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 88.6-mile (142.6 km) path featuring significant elevation gain through the region's rolling hills.

Q: Where can I find parking or access routes via public transport in Gimont?
A: Gimont, as a town, offers various parking options, particularly around the town center. For public transport, Gimont has a train station, making it accessible by rail. You can find more information about the station and its services on the &lt;a href="https://www.visit-occitanie.com/en/rail-tour/gares-etapes/gimont/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Occitanie website&lt;/a&gt;, which can help with planning your access to the cycling routes.

Q: Can I find food and accommodation options near the cycling routes in Gimont?
A: Gimont is a charming bastide town with cultural and historical attractions, including a market hall and local restaurants. You'll find various amenities within the town itself, making it a convenient base for cyclists looking for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation after a day on the trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options from Gimont?
A: Yes, the region offers routes that provide a substantial distance for touring. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021110117/loop-of-the-medieval-villages-of-sarrant-and-hom" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of the medieval villages of Sarrant and Hom&lt;/a&gt; covers 51.8 miles (83.3 km), exploring historic villages within the Gascony countryside.
tips: Pretty medieval village. The old guard path allows you to discover old sculptures and statues.

Medieval village classified among the "most beautiful villages of France". You enter the village through a very beautiful arched doorway to discover small streets lined with tall, colorful stone and half-timbered houses built around the Saint-Vincent church. Do not miss the bookstore which is a nugget and which serves delicious sandwiches!

faqs.question: How challenging is the 'Medieval village of Sarrant' cycling loop?
faqs.answer: This 83.3-kilometer loop is classified as a "hard bike ride." It demands very good fitness due to a significant cumulative elevation gain of 900 meters over rolling, hilly terrain. While individual slopes may not be extremely steep, the continuous ascents and descents require sustained effort.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route immerses cyclists in the quintessential Gascon countryside, featuring panoramic views of verdant hills, expansive sunflower fields (seasonally), and Armagnac vineyards. The terrain is characterized by rolling hills, often described as an "egg carton" topography, with frequent but not abrupt changes in elevation. You'll primarily ride on paved surfaces along quiet country lanes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The focal point is the medieval village of Sarrant, recognized as one of "The Most Beautiful Villages in France" and the "Village de l'Illustration®." You can explore its unique circular layout, half-timbered houses, and 14th-century gate. The route also passes through the Medieval village of Homps and offers glimpses of impressive castles and charming fortified villages, providing a rich historical and cultural immersion.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Gascon countryside is particularly beautiful in spring and early summer when the fields are green and wildflowers bloom, or later in summer for sunflower fields. However, be mindful of summer heat. Autumn also offers pleasant cycling conditions with cooler temperatures and changing foliage. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Sarrant cycling loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking within or near the medieval village of Sarrant itself, as it serves as the starting and focal point for the loop. Look for designated parking areas in the village.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach Sarrant?
faqs.answer: Sarrant is a rural village in Gascony, and public transport options are limited. It is generally recommended to reach Sarrant by car, especially if you are bringing a touring bicycle. Check local bus schedules for the Gers department, but direct connections might be infrequent.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a touring bicycle route primarily on paved country roads. While dogs are generally welcome in the French countryside, cycling with a dog for 83.3 kilometers on a difficult route might be challenging. If your dog is accustomed to long rides in a trailer or basket, it could be feasible, but consider the distance and elevation. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It uses public roads and paths through the Gascon countryside. Access to the village of Sarrant is also free, though some specific attractions or museums within the village might have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several small villages where you may find local cafes, bakeries (boulangeries), or restaurants, especially in Sarrant itself. However, given the rural nature of Gascony, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and some snacks, as services might be sparse between villages. Always check opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist season.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not recommended for families with young children or beginner cyclists. It is classified as a "hard bike ride" due to its significant distance (83.3 km) and cumulative elevation gain (900 m). It requires very good fitness and experience with long-distance touring cycling.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other local paths, including Le Grand Tour par Giscaro, Le sentier des pigeonniers à Sarrant, Au Bois de Tulle, and Vues sur Mauvezin.
